input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<CollectionView
    ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
    SelectionMode="Single"
    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <CollectionView.Header>
        <RefreshView
            Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
            IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
        </RefreshView>
    </CollectionView.Header>

    <!-- Wrap the whole CollectionView in a RefreshView instead: -->
</CollectionView>
```

The standard pattern is to **wrap** the `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView
    Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView
        ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
        SelectionMode="Single"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}">

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>

    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<MyItem> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private MyItem? _selectedItem;

    // React to selection changes — navigate to detail page
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(MyItem? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;

        _nav.NavigateAsync("DetailPage", new { Id = value.Id });

        // Clear selection so the user can re-select the same item later
        SelectedItem = null;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var fresh = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<MyItem>(fresh);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false; // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Direction | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource → Items` | OneWay | Drives the list from your `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectedItem ↔ SelectedItem` | **TwoWay** | VM receives taps; setting it to `null` clears the highlight |
| `IsRefreshing ↔ IsRefreshing` | TwoWay | VM sets `false` to dismiss the spinner |
| `Command → RefreshCommand` | OneWay | `RefreshView` invokes this on pull-down |

**Important notes:**
- `SelectionMode="Single"` — use `None` to disable, `Multiple` for multi-select.
- Always set `IsRefreshing = false` in a `finally` block, or the spinner hangs on errors.
- Reset `SelectedItem = null` after navigation so the same row can be tapped again.
- This uses the **MVVM Community Toolkit** (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`). Without it, implement `INotifyPropertyChanged` manually.
